measurement_note: Size D is floor(sqrt(x)), the number of possible small-divisor positions for the single input value x. Prime cubes exercise the complete square-root scan while still having exactly four divisors. The 11/70/225 tiers span more than 4x. The reference performs O(D) divisor tests; a correct slower implementation enumerates all candidates through x, which is quadratic in D for these values, and completes normally.
